title: Knowledge Voyage 2 - How GPS Knows Where You Are
description: The V3 explainer redeems the 'teach the real terms' note: a zero-to-understanding voyage through how your phone finds you - trilateration (not triangulation), atomic clocks, turning time into distance, and the showstopper - the special- and general-relativity time dilation (+45 and -7 = +38 microseconds/day) that GPS must correct or drift ~10 km/day. Plus ephemeris vs almanac and the GNSS family (GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou). Anchored in Captain's eBird coordinates: every checklist pin placed by four atomic clocks and a dead physicist riding shotgun.
